title: Ukraine Strikes Russia's Ust-Luga Baltic Oil Port — Largest Drone Attack of 2026
detail: Ukraine launched its largest overnight drone attack of 2026 on the night of March 24–25, striking Russia's Ust-Luga oil export port on the Baltic Sea (150 km southwest of St. Petersburg). Drones hit Novatek's oil-product facilities at the port at 3:43 a.m. local time, setting ablaze storage tanks and loading equipment and halting crude loadings. Governor Alexander Drozdenko said 56 drones were intercepted in Leningrad region; Russia's Defense Ministry said 389 Ukrainian drones were intercepted overnight — the highest number since March 10, 2025. Ust-Luga ships approximately 450,000 barrels of crude per day. The Kronstadt naval base area also sustained property damage. Ukraine also struck the Novatek Gas Facility in Russia's Leningrad Region. Approximately 40% of Russia's oil export capacity was halted amid Ukrainian strikes, pipeline damage, and tanker seizures, according to Reuters.

media.caption: Ukraine hits Ust-Luga oil terminal in largest overnight drone attack of the year

title: Russian Drone Strikes 14-Storey Residential Building in Dnipro
detail: A Russian drone struck a 14-storey residential building in Dnipro on March 25, causing injuries. Ukraine's Armed Forces also struck a Bastion Coastal Missile System in Crimea on this day, the launcher type associated with Zircon hypersonic missiles. Russia's Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu separately acknowledged that 'not a single region in Russia can consider itself safe,' noting that even the Ural region is 'firmly in the zone of constant threat' from Ukrainian strikes.

media.caption: Russian drone slams into Dnipro apartment block during massive air attack

title: 40% of Russia's Oil Export Capacity Halted Amid Sustained Ukrainian Drone Campaign
detail: Reuters reported on March 25 that approximately 40% of Russia's oil export capacity had been halted due to a combination of Ukrainian drone strikes on port infrastructure, pipeline damage, and tanker seizures. Affected terminals include Ust-Luga and Primorsk on the Baltic Sea. Ukraine's drone campaign has effectively doubled its pace in early 2026, with about four targets struck every night. Russia's Security Council Secretary Shoigu admitted no Russian region is safe from Ukrainian strikes. Ukraine's strategic goal is to curtail Kremlin oil revenue financing the invasion and reduce fuel supplies to frontline Russian forces.

media.caption: Ukrainian drone strikes halt at least 40% of Russia's oil export capacity
